entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A staple crop (Pennisetum glaucum) used for food security in Africa and Asia.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: The most important production constraint for pearl millet in specific regions.

entities.name: SUB-SAHARA AFRICA
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is a staple diet.

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A region where pearl millet is a staple diet.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of improving pearl millet for drought-prone areas in resource-limited ecosystems.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to withstand and produce under drought conditions.

entities.name: ADAPTATION MECHANISM
entities.description: The biological response of pearl millet to drought situations at different growth stages.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of developing cultivars with enhanced drought tolerance.

entities.name: GENOMIC TOOLS
entities.description: Advanced tools used to improve the efficiency of breeding for drought tolerance.

entities.name: EARLY MATURING
entities.description: A characteristic of cultivars developed to avoid late-season drought.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A productivity measure used as a selection criterion for releasing drought-tolerant cultivars.

entities.name: STOVER YIELD
entities.description: A productivity measure used as a selection criterion for releasing drought-tolerant cultivars.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE TRAIT LOCI
entities.description: Genetic regions associated with the trait of drought tolerance.
